Description:
Build the clinician-facing participant management page at /participants.

Current state: Stubbed page with 'Coming in a future sprint' text.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Table view with columns: Name, Program, Current Module, Homework Status (badge: Complete/Partial/Not Started), Last Active, Status Indicator
- Status indicator: green dot (on-track: homework >=80%, active in 48h), amber (behind: homework <80% or inactive 3+ days), red (needs attention: inactive 7+ days)
- Search/filter by program
- Click row navigates to participant detail page

Participant Detail sub-page (/participants/[id]):
- Left column (2/3): current enrollment info, module progress timeline, homework detail for current module, session history
- Right column (1/3): quick actions (Unlock Next Module, Push a Task, Schedule Session), SMART goals, recent shared journal entries
- Enrollment management: pause, drop, reset module progress

API work needed:
- GET /api/clinician/participants — list all participants across clinician's programs with aggregated status
- GET /api/clinician/participants/:id — detailed view with enrollment, progress, journal, homework data

Dependencies: Enrollment system (done), Module progress tracking (done)

Full session scheduling, completion, and session-gated module unlock flow.

Current state: Session model exists in Prisma schema. Sessions page is stubbed. No API routes for sessions.

API:
- POST /api/sessions — create session (clinician). Auto-creates CalendarEvent for participant with eventType=SESSION
- GET /api/sessions — list sessions for clinician (filterable by date range, enrollmentId)
- PUT /api/sessions/:id — update (reschedule, change video link)
- PUT /api/sessions/:id/complete — mark completed with clinicianNotes, modulesCompleted array, tasks to push. Triggers checkAndUnlockNextModule()
- GET /api/sessions/upcoming (participant) — next scheduled session
- GET /api/sessions/history (participant) — past sessions

Web (CAS):
- Sessions page: calendar/list view of all scheduled sessions across participants
- On participant detail page, Sessions tab: schedule session dialog (date/time picker, video URL), list of past sessions
- Complete Session dialog: clinician notes, share toggle, module completed dropdown, tasks to assign
- Prepare for Session full-screen view: Steady Work Review, homework status, quick stats, last session notes, intended outcomes

Mobile:
- Sessions appear on participant calendar
- Session card: time, clinician name, video call link, pre-session checklist
- Join Call button

Dependencies: Calendar (done), Enrollments (done), Module progress (done)

Implement the TIME_BASED and MANUAL unlock rules for modules.

Current state: SEQUENTIAL unlock works. TIME_BASED and MANUAL are defined in enum but not implemented. Logic in packages/api/src/routes/participant.ts lines 272-304.

Acceptance Criteria:
- When a module has unlockRule: TIME_BASED and unlockDelayDays: N, module auto-unlocks N days after previous module completed
- Background job (or check on enrollment fetch) evaluates unlock eligibility
- MANUAL unlock: clinician-only endpoint PUT /api/enrollments/:id/modules/:moduleId/unlock
- Add Quick Unlock button on participant module timeline in CAS

Dependencies: Module progress (done)

Stats aggregation service + clinician analytics + participant insights screen.

Current state: No stats service or endpoints. Only basic enrollment counting.

API — Stats service (packages/api/src/services/stats.ts):
- getTaskCompletionRate(participantId, dateRange)
- getTimeEstimationAccuracy(participantId, dateRange)
- getJournalingConsistency(participantId, dateRange)
- getHomeworkCompletionRate(enrollmentId)
- getRegulationTrend(participantId, dateRange) — array of { date, score }
- getSystemCheckinAdherence(participantId, dateRange)
- GET /api/stats/participant (participant) — own stats, default last 4 weeks
- GET /api/stats/participant/:participantId (clinician)

CAS — Patterns tab on participant detail:
- Task completion bar chart (weekly)
- Regulation trend line chart
- Journaling consistency calendar heatmap
- Homework completion per-module progress bars
- Highlight concerning trends

Mobile — Insights screen:
- Accessible from home screen card
- Same charts using react-native-chart-kit or victory-native
- Earth tone palette

Dependencies: Tasks (done), Journal (done), Module progress (done)

Guided onboarding for new participants after accepting enrollment invitation.

Current state: ParticipantProfile.onboardingCompleted field exists but is unused. No onboarding screens.

Acceptance Criteria:
- After first login / accepting invite, participant sees onboarding flow
- Steps: profile setup (name, timezone), notification preferences (morning check-in time, reminder preferences), calendar sync prompt, Steady System intro
- Mark onboardingCompleted = true when done
- Skip option available
- Gate main app behind onboarding (redirect if not completed)

Dependencies: Auth (done), Notification preferences (done)

Pre-session review questionnaire where participants reflect on homework and identify barriers.

Current state: Not implemented. No DB model, no API, no UI.

DB:
- New SteadyWorkReview model: (id, enrollmentId, moduleId, responses JSON, barriers String[], submittedAt)

API:
- POST /api/enrollments/:id/reviews — submit review
- GET /api/enrollments/:id/reviews — list reviews

CAS:
- Program settings: toggle Enable Steady Work Review, editable review questions (defaults: What did you complete?, What worked well?, What didn't work?, How much time spent?)
- Barrier diagnostic checklist: editable defaults (Wasn't written down, Wasn't scheduled, Activation stalled, Time estimation failed, The future didn't feel real, Steps were unclear, Prospective memory broke down, Depended on someone else, Forgot)
- Visible in session prep view

Mobile:
- Notification 24h before session: Time to complete your Steady Work Review
- Review screen: questions with text inputs, barrier multi-select checklist, submit
- Accessible from session card on calendar

Dependencies: Sessions (ticket #2), Enrollments (done)

Allow clinicians to customize homework per participant — hide items, add items, add resources, add notes.

Current state: Not implemented.

DB:
- New EnrollmentOverride model: (id, enrollmentId, moduleId, partId?, overrideType ENUM [HIDE_HOMEWORK_ITEM, ADD_HOMEWORK_ITEM, ADD_RESOURCE, CLINICIAN_NOTE], overrideData JSON, createdAt)

API:

CAS — Customize tab on participant detail:

Dependencies: Homework builder (done), Enrollments (done)

Two-way sync between participant's Google Calendar and Steady calendar.

Current state: CalendarEvent model has externalCalendarId field. No sync logic.

DB:
- New CalendarSync model: (id, userId, provider ENUM [GOOGLE, APPLE, OUTLOOK], externalCalendarId, syncEnabled, lastSyncAt)

API:
- Google OAuth2 flow, store refresh tokens encrypted
- Pull: fetch events from Google Calendar for next 30 days, create/update CalendarEvents with eventType=EXTERNAL_SYNC
- Push: app-created events pushed to Google Calendar
- Sync: on-demand + every 15 min via background job
- Conflict resolution: external calendar = source of truth for external events, app for app events

Mobile:
- Settings > Calendar Sync: Connect Calendar button, OAuth flow
- External events in distinct color (light gray) with Google icon
- Apple/Outlook: Coming soon labels

Dependencies: Calendar (done)

Pre-signed S3 URL flow for file uploads (handouts, images, attachments).

Current state: coverImageUrl and resourceUrl fields exist but no upload infrastructure. Per CLAUDE.md: API never touches file bytes.

Acceptance Criteria:
- S3 bucket with encryption at rest, HTTPS-only, no public URLs (HIPAA)
- POST /api/uploads/presign — returns pre-signed upload URL + final URL. Params: { fileName, fileType, context }
- GET /api/uploads/presign-download — returns pre-signed download URL
- CAS: file upload component that uploads directly to S3 via pre-signed URL, stores S3 key
- Use in: program cover images, homework resource handouts (PDFs), future attachments

Dependencies: None

Generate downloadable PDFs for program summaries and progress reports.

Current state: Not implemented.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Program summary PDF: title, all modules, all parts (text content, homework listed)
- Participant progress report PDF: enrollment status, module completion, homework status, assessment scores, regulation trend
- GET /api/programs/:id/export/pdf (clinician)
- GET /api/enrollments/:id/report/pdf (clinician)
- Use @react-pdf/renderer or puppeteer
- Generate as background job if >200ms, return download URL

Dependencies: Programs (done), Analytics (ticket #4)

Log all data mutations with userId, timestamp, resource type, and action for HIPAA compliance.

Current state: Not implemented. CLAUDE.md mentions Prisma middleware approach.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Prisma middleware that intercepts all create/update/delete operations
- Log to AuditLog table: (id, userId, action ENUM [CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE], resourceType, resourceId, metadata JSON, timestamp)
- Never log PII at INFO level — only IDs and operation names
- GET /api/admin/audit-logs — admin-only, filterable by userId, resourceType, action, date range, cursor-based pagination
- Retention policy: configurable, default 7 years

Dependencies: Auth (done)

Wire up existing notification infrastructure with session/homework lifecycle scheduling.

Current state: Expo push sending works. pg-boss queue exists. Morning check-in and weekly review workers defined. Not fully integrated.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Session reminders: 3 notifications per session (24h, 1h, 10min before) — auto-scheduled on session creation
- Homework reminders: based on reminderCadence, only sent if homework not completed
- Morning check-in: daily at participant preferred time, rotating warm copy
- Weekly review: weekly at preferred day/time
- Notification preferences respected (category toggles, preferred times)
- Cancel reminders when homework completed or session cancelled
- Smart escalation: after 3+ dismissals in 7 days, switch to diagnostic prompt copy

Dependencies: Sessions (ticket #2), Notification preferences (done), Queue (done)

Participants can invite an accountability partner who receives weekly aggregate summaries.

Current state: Not implemented.

DB:
- AccountabilityPartner model: (id, participantId, partnerName, partnerEmail, inviteCode UNIQUE, inviteStatus ENUM [PENDING, ACCEPTED, REVOKED], createdAt)
- PartnerWeeklySummary model: (id, partnerId, weekStartDate, tasksCompleted, totalTasks, journalEntries, nextSessionDate?, sentAt)

API:
- POST /api/partners/invite — generate invite, send email
- GET /api/partners — list partners
- DELETE /api/partners/:id — revoke
- GET /api/partners/summary/:inviteCode — public (no auth), aggregate only. NEVER task/journal content or clinical data
- Background job: weekly summary generation + email every Sunday

Mobile:
- Settings > Accountability Partners: invite form, partner list, remove button
- Privacy note: partner sees weekly summary only, never details

Dependencies: Tasks (done), Journal (done)

Completion animations, streaks with milestones, and module celebration overlays.

Current state: Basic task completion with optimistic UI. No animations or streak badges.

Acceptance Criteria:

- Streak badges on home screen: journaling, app check-in, homework. Forgive 1 gap day per 7-day window logic
- Milestone popups at 7, 14, 21, 30 days: celebratory bottom sheet
- Module completion: full-screen overlay with confetti (react-native-confetti-cannon), module title, clinician message or default

Dependencies: Stats service (ticket #4)

AsyncStorage persistence for React Query cache + offline mutation queue.
